features.name: Spaced Repetition System
features.description: Schedule each word for review at increasing intervals based on answer correctness. Incorrect answers reset the interval; correct answers extend it (e.g. 1d → 3d → 7d → 14d → 30d).
features.currentState: No persistence of results or scheduling. Quiz results exist only in session state.

backend: Add a UserWordProgress schema: { userId, wordId, interval, nextReviewAt, easeFactor, consecutiveCorrect }
POST /progress endpoint to record a quiz result and compute the next review date
GET /words/due endpoint returning words where nextReviewAt <= now for the authenticated user
frontend: Replace localStorage word list with API-fetched due words
Submit results to POST /progress after each session

acceptanceCriteria: A word answered correctly three times in a row is not shown again for at least 7 days
A word answered incorrectly resets to a 1-day interval
The due-word count is shown to the user before starting a session

features.name: Difficult Words Queue
features.description: Surface words with 2+ consecutive incorrect answers into a dedicated review session available at any time, independent of the SRS schedule.
features.currentState: Per-session incorrect answers are tracked in useTimedQuizMachine but not persisted.

backend: Add consecutiveIncorrect counter to UserWordProgress
GET /words/difficult endpoint returning words where consecutiveIncorrect >= 2
frontend: Add 'Difficult Words' mode option on the quiz start screen
Feed difficult words into the existing quiz machine

acceptanceCriteria: Words answered incorrectly twice in a row appear in the Difficult Words list
Answering a difficult word correctly twice removes it from the list
The difficult word count is shown on the home/dashboard screen

features.name: Streak Tracking
features.description: Track consecutive days on which the user completes at least one quiz session. Display the streak count prominently. Reset to 0 if a calendar day is missed.

backend: Add streakCount and lastActivityDate fields to the User schema
Update streak on any POST /progress call: increment if lastActivityDate was yesterday, reset to 1 if older, no-op if today
frontend: Display streak count in the header or home screen
Show a celebration animation when the streak increments

acceptanceCriteria: Completing a session on consecutive days increments the streak
Missing a calendar day resets the streak to 0
The current streak is visible without starting a session

features.name: Progress Dashboard & Statistics
features.description: A dedicated page showing total words learned, accuracy over the last 7 and 30 days, a daily activity heatmap, and per-word history.

backend: GET /stats endpoint returning aggregate metrics for the authenticated user
Store per-session summary (date, correctCount, incorrectCount) in a Sessions collection
frontend: New /progress route with chart components (e.g. recharts or Chart.js)
Activity heatmap component showing daily session completion

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows total unique words with at least one correct answer
Accuracy percentage is shown for the last 7 and 30 days
A heatmap shows which days had activity in the last 3 months

features.name: Multiple Quiz Modes
features.description: Offer distinct learning modes: Classic (untimed multiple-choice), Speed Review (current timed mode), Typing Practice (user types translation), Flashcard (self-reported correct/incorrect).

frontend: Mode selector on the quiz start screen
TypingPractice component: text input with exact-match or fuzzy-match checking
Flashcard component: show word → flip → self-report
Classic mode: remove countdown timer from existing TimedVocabularyTest

acceptanceCriteria: User can select a mode before starting a session
Typing mode accepts the correct translation (case-insensitive) and flags common typos
Flashcard mode records self-reported outcome and feeds it into SRS

features.name: Audio Pronunciation
features.description: Play a native-speaker audio clip for each word during review. Allow the user to replay on demand. Support a listening-only quiz mode where audio plays first and the user identifies the word.
features.currentState: No audio field on Word schema; no playback implemented.

backend: Add audioUrl field to Word schema
Support audio file upload (S3 or local storage) via POST /words/:id/audio
frontend: Audio player component (HTML5 <audio> or Howler.js)
Auto-play audio when a new question is shown
Listening mode: play audio, show four word options

acceptanceCriteria: Audio plays automatically when a question loads (if available)
A replay button replays the clip
Words without audio degrade gracefully (no broken UI)

features.name: Example Sentences
features.description: Display a short Uzbek example sentence using the target word alongside its translation, giving learners context beyond a bare word-translation pair.
features.currentState: Word schema has no exampleSentence field.

backend: Add exampleSentence?: string and exampleSentenceTranslation?: string to Word schema and DTOs
frontend: Show example sentence below the word during the answer reveal phase

acceptanceCriteria: Words with an example sentence display it during the answer reveal
Words without an example sentence show nothing extra (no empty placeholder)

features.name: Daily Goal Setting
features.description: Let users choose a daily target (5, 10, 15, or 20 words) and show progress toward that goal within the session and on the home screen.

backend: Add dailyGoal field to User schema (default 10)
frontend: Goal picker in user settings
Progress bar on home screen: X / dailyGoal words reviewed today

acceptanceCriteria: User can change their daily goal from settings
Home screen shows today's progress toward the goal
Reaching the goal shows a completion indicator

features.name: Learning Reminders / Notifications
features.description: Send a browser push notification or email at a user-configured time each day if the user has not yet completed their daily session.

backend: Store reminderTime and reminderEnabled in User schema
Scheduled job (cron) to send Web Push or email to users who have not yet met their daily goal
frontend: Request notification permission on opt-in
Store Push API subscription in backend
Reminder settings in user preferences page

acceptanceCriteria: User can enable/disable reminders and choose a time
Notification is not sent if the daily goal is already met
Disabling reminders stops all future notifications immediately

features.name: Leaderboards & Social Features
features.description: Rank users by weekly XP points earned from correct answers. Show a global leaderboard and an optional friends leaderboard.
features.currentState: Not implemented. Requires a points/XP system as prerequisite.

backend: Add weeklyXp and totalXp to User schema
Award XP on POST /progress based on correctness and word difficulty
GET /leaderboard endpoint returning top N users by weeklyXp
Weekly cron to reset weeklyXp
frontend: Leaderboard page /leaderboard
XP counter in header

acceptanceCriteria: Correct answers award XP; incorrect answers award 0
Leaderboard resets weekly
User can see their own rank even if outside the top 10

features.name: User-Created Mnemonics (Mems)
features.description: Allow users to attach a text or image mnemonic to any word. Other users can upvote mems. The highest-voted mem is shown during review.

backend: New Mem schema: { id, wordId, authorId, text, imageUrl?, upvotes: number }
CRUD endpoints for mems under /words/:id/mems
POST /words/:id/mems/:memId/upvote
frontend: Mem display during answer reveal
Mem creation form accessible from the word detail view
Upvote button on each mem

acceptanceCriteria: Any authenticated user can create a mem for any word
The mem with the most upvotes is shown by default during review
Users cannot upvote their own mem

features.name: Offline Mode
features.description: Ensure the quiz remains fully functional without connectivity, and queue SRS result submissions for sync when the connection is restored.
features.currentState: Quiz already works offline via localStorage. The gap is result sync and new-word fetch.

frontend: Service Worker (or next-pwa) to cache the app shell and word data
IndexedDB queue for unsynchronised progress events; flush on reconnect

acceptanceCriteria: Quiz runs without any network requests once words are loaded
Progress is queued locally and synced automatically when back online
User sees a clear offline indicator in the UI
